name: Rose
description: One of the most beloved flowering plants in the world, known for its beautiful blooms and fragrance.

care.water: Water deeply twice a week, allowing soil to dry slightly between waterings.
care.sunlight: Full sun, at least 6 hours of direct sunlight daily.
care.fertilizer: Feed every 4–6 weeks during growing season with a balanced fertilizer.

name: Snake Plant
description: A hardy, air-purifying houseplant with striking upright leaves. Nearly indestructible and perfect for beginners.

care.water: Water every 2–6 weeks, depending on light. Allow soil to dry completely.
care.sunlight: Tolerates low light to bright indirect sunlight.
care.soil: Well-draining sandy or cactus mix soil.
care.fertilizer: Feed monthly during spring and summer with diluted fertilizer.

name: Mango Tree
description: A tropical fruit tree prized for its sweet, juicy fruits. One of the most cultivated fruits in the tropics.

care.water: Water deeply once or twice a week when young; mature trees need less frequent watering.
care.fertilizer: Apply balanced fertilizer 3 times a year.

name: Money Plant
description: A popular trailing houseplant believed to bring good luck and fortune. Extremely easy to grow.

care.water: Water when the top inch of soil is dry, typically every 1–2 weeks.
care.sunlight: Bright indirect light; can tolerate low light.
care.fertilizer: Monthly feeding during growing season.

name: Bamboo
description: One of the fastest-growing plants on Earth. Used for construction, food, and decoration.

care.water: Water deeply 2–3 times per week when establishing; once weekly when mature.
care.fertilizer: Apply nitrogen-rich fertilizer in spring and summer.

name: Aloe Vera
description: A medicinal succulent known for its soothing gel used to treat burns and skin irritations.

care.water: Water every 3 weeks in summer, every 6–8 weeks in winter.
care.sunlight: Bright indirect sunlight; can handle direct sun if acclimated.
care.soil: Sandy, well-draining cactus or succulent mix.
care.fertilizer: Once a year in spring with a succulent fertilizer.

name: Sunflower
description: Iconic bright yellow flowers that track the sun. Grows tall and produces edible seeds.

care.water: Water at the base once a week; more during hot summer months.
care.sunlight: Full sun, at least 6–8 hours daily.
care.soil: Well-draining, slightly acidic to neutral soil.
care.fertilizer: Light feeding; too much nitrogen causes leafy growth over flowers.

name: Coconut Palm
description: The tree of life in tropical regions. Provides food, water, oil, and building materials.

care.water: Water regularly; requires high humidity and well-distributed rainfall.
care.sunlight: Full sun, at least 6 hours daily.
care.soil: Sandy, well-draining soil near coastal areas.
care.fertilizer: Apply slow-release fertilizer 3–4 times annually.

name: Peace Lily
description: Elegant white-blooming houseplant known for air purification and low-light tolerance.

care.water: Keep soil moist but not waterlogged; water when leaves droop slightly.
care.fertilizer: Feed monthly in spring and summer.

name: Orchid
description: One of the largest flowering plant families. Known for exotic, long-lasting blooms.

care.water: Water every 7–10 days; allow roots to dry between waterings.
care.sunlight: Bright indirect light; avoid direct afternoon sun.
care.soil: Orchid bark mix with good drainage.
care.fertilizer: Diluted orchid fertilizer weekly during growing season.

name: Banana Plant
description: A fast-growing tropical plant bearing clusters of sweet fruits. Also used ornamentally.

care.water: Water deeply and regularly; needs consistent moisture.
care.soil: Rich, well-draining soil with high organic matter.
care.fertilizer: Heavy feeder; fertilize monthly with balanced fertilizer.

name: Fern
description: Ancient vascular plants with feathery fronds. Thrives in moist, shaded environments.

care.water: Keep soil consistently moist; mist leaves regularly for humidity.
care.sunlight: Indirect to low light; avoid direct sun.
care.fertilizer: Feed monthly in spring and summer with diluted fertilizer.

name: Lavender
description: A fragrant herb and flowering plant prized for its calming scent and purple blooms.

care.water: Water once or twice a week; drought-tolerant once established.
care.fertilizer: Light fertilizer in spring; too much causes leafy growth.

name: Cactus
description: Desert plants adapted to extremely dry conditions. Stores water in thick stems.

care.water: Water sparingly; every 2–3 weeks in summer, once a month or less in winter.
care.fertilizer: Feed once in spring with cactus fertilizer.

location: Arid / Desert Outdoor or Sunny Indoor

name: Jade Plant
description: A popular succulent houseplant resembling a miniature tree. Associated with good luck.

care.water: Water thoroughly, then allow soil to dry completely between waterings.
care.sunlight: Bright direct or indirect sunlight, 4+ hours daily.
care.fertilizer: Feed 2–3 times a year with balanced fertilizer.

name: Monstera
description: Iconic tropical plant with large, split leaves. A design favorite for modern interiors.

care.water: Water when top 2 inches of soil are dry, typically every 1–2 weeks.
care.fertilizer: Monthly fertilizer during spring and summer.

name: Basil
description: An aromatic culinary herb essential in many cuisines worldwide.

care.water: Water regularly, keeping soil moist but not soggy.
care.fertilizer: Feed every 2–3 weeks with nitrogen-rich fertilizer.

name: Cherry Blossom
description: Japan's iconic flowering tree producing spectacular pink blooms in spring.

care.water: Water deeply once a week; more frequently in hot weather.
care.fertilizer: Apply balanced fertilizer in early spring before blooming.

name: Pothos
description: A resilient trailing houseplant that thrives in almost any condition.

care.water: Allow soil to dry between waterings, every 1–2 weeks.
care.fertilizer: Monthly in spring and summer.

name: Papaya
description: A fast-growing tropical fruit tree bearing large, sweet orange fruits.

care.water: Water regularly; drought-sensitive but avoid waterlogging.
care.fertilizer: Monthly balanced fertilizer; high in nitrogen.

name: Spider Plant
description: A cheerful, air-purifying houseplant producing cascading spiderettes.

care.water: Water moderately; allow soil to dry slightly between waterings.
care.sunlight: Indirect bright light; tolerates low light.
care.fertilizer: Monthly in spring and summer.

name: Hibiscus
description: A tropical flowering shrub with large, vibrant blooms. The national flower of Malaysia.

care.water: Water deeply and regularly; 1 inch per week.
care.fertilizer: Feed every 2 weeks in growing season with high-potassium fertilizer.

name: Ginger
description: A tropical rhizome plant with culinary and medicinal uses.

care.water: Keep soil consistently moist; water 2–3 times a week.
care.fertilizer: Monthly balanced fertilizer during growing season.

name: Fiddle Leaf Fig
description: A statement indoor tree with large, violin-shaped leaves beloved in interior design.

care.water: Water when top inch of soil is dry; avoid overwatering.
care.sunlight: Bright indirect light; avoid direct afternoon sun.
care.fertilizer: Monthly during spring and summer.

name: Narra Tree
description: The national tree of the Philippines, known for its hard wood and yellow flowers.

care.water: Moderate watering; drought-tolerant once established.
care.fertilizer: Light feeding; generally low-maintenance.

name: Sampaguita
description: The national flower of the Philippines. Small white flowers with an intensely sweet fragrance.

care.water: Water regularly to keep soil moist.
care.fertilizer: Monthly feeding with balanced fertilizer.

name: Acacia
description: A fast-growing leguminous tree widely used in reforestation and timber production.

care.water: Drought-tolerant; water during establishment.
care.soil: Adaptable to a wide range of soils including poor soils.
care.fertilizer: Minimal; nitrogen-fixing roots reduce need.

name: ZZ Plant
description: A virtually indestructible houseplant with glossy, waxy leaves. Handles neglect well.

care.water: Water every 2–3 weeks; very drought-tolerant.
care.fertilizer: Feed 2–3 times a year.

name: Tulip
description: Elegant cup-shaped flowers blooming in spring, available in virtually every color.

care.water: Moderate watering; allow soil to dry between waterings.
care.fertilizer: Apply bulb fertilizer at planting and in early spring.

name: Mint
description: A fast-spreading aromatic herb used in cooking, beverages, and medicine.

care.water: Keep soil consistently moist; water every 1–2 days.
care.fertilizer: Monthly with balanced fertilizer.

name: Calamondin
description: A small citrus tree popular in the Philippines. Sour fruits used in local cuisine and beverages.

care.water: Water regularly; keep soil moist but well-drained.
care.fertilizer: Feed monthly with citrus fertilizer.

name: Lucky Bamboo
description: A popular good-luck plant often grown in water or pebbles. Not true bamboo.

care.water: Change water every 7–10 days if grown in water; keep soil moist if in soil.
care.soil: Well-draining potting mix or water with pebbles.
care.fertilizer: Diluted liquid fertilizer monthly.

name: Bougainvillea
description: A vibrant tropical climbing plant with brilliant papery bracts in magenta, purple, and orange.

care.water: Drought-tolerant; water deeply then allow to dry.
care.fertilizer: Monthly with high-phosphorus fertilizer to encourage blooming.

name: Tomato
description: A widely cultivated fruiting vegetable plant. A staple in gardens and kitchens worldwide.

care.water: Water deeply 1–2 inches per week; consistent moisture prevents cracking.
care.fertilizer: Feed every 2 weeks with tomato-specific fertilizer.

name: Bird of Paradise
description: A dramatic tropical plant with bold orange and blue flowers resembling a bird in flight.

care.water: Water every 1–2 weeks; allow top half of soil to dry.
care.fertilizer: Monthly during spring and summer.

name: Rubber Plant
description: A bold indoor tree with large, waxy leaves in dark green or burgundy.

care.water: Water every 1–2 weeks; allow soil to dry between waterings.
care.fertilizer: Monthly in growing season.

name: Calamansi
description: A small citrus fruit native to the Philippines, widely used as a condiment and souring agent.

care.water: Regular watering; needs consistent moisture.
care.fertilizer: Monthly citrus fertilizer.

name: String of Pearls
description: A unique trailing succulent with tiny spherical leaves resembling a string of beads.

care.water: Water sparingly every 2–3 weeks; very drought-tolerant.
care.fertilizer: Once in spring with diluted succulent fertilizer.

name: Guava
description: A tropical fruit tree producing fragrant, nutritious fruits rich in vitamin C.

care.water: Water regularly during dry season; drought-tolerant once established.
care.fertilizer: Monthly fertilizer during growing season.

name: Pandan
description: A tropical plant with fragrant leaves used extensively in Southeast Asian cooking.

care.water: Keep consistently moist; water 2–3 times a week.
care.sunlight: Partial shade to bright indirect light.
care.fertilizer: Monthly with balanced fertilizer.

name: Anthurium
description: A tropical plant with glossy, heart-shaped red spathes. Long-lasting and dramatic.

care.water: Water every 1–2 weeks; keep soil slightly moist.
care.soil: Chunky, well-draining orchid or aroid mix.
care.fertilizer: Monthly with phosphorus-rich fertilizer.

name: Dracaena
description: A dramatic, low-maintenance plant with spiky leaves. An excellent air purifier.

care.water: Water every 1–2 weeks; sensitive to fluoride in tap water.
care.fertilizer: Feed monthly in spring and summer.

name: Sweet Potato Vine
description: A nutritious tropical root crop widely grown in the Philippines.

care.water: Moderate watering; drought-tolerant once established.
care.fertilizer: Light feeding; too much nitrogen reduces root production.

name: Ylang-Ylang
description: A fragrant tropical tree whose flowers are used in perfume production and aromatherapy.

care.water: Regular watering; moisture-loving.
care.fertilizer: Monthly during growing season.

name: Kalanchoe
description: A cheerful flowering succulent with clusters of small, long-lasting blooms.

care.water: Allow soil to dry between waterings; every 2–3 weeks.
care.fertilizer: Monthly during bloom period with low-nitrogen fertilizer.

name: Mahogany
description: A valuable timber tree native to the Americas, widely planted in the Philippines.

care.water: Regular watering when young; drought-tolerant once established.
care.fertilizer: Minimal once established.

name: Venus Flytrap
description: A fascinating carnivorous plant that catches and digests insects and spiders.

care.water: Use distilled or rainwater; keep soil moist via tray method.
care.soil: Low-nutrient sphagnum moss or peat mix.
care.fertilizer: Never fertilize; feed live insects instead.

name: Lipstick Plant
description: A tropical vine with tubular red flowers that emerge from dark tubes, resembling a lipstick.

care.water: Water when top inch of soil is dry; needs humidity.
care.fertilizer: Monthly in spring and summer.

name: Pineapple Plant
description: A tropical bromeliad producing the iconic pineapple fruit.

care.water: Water moderately; drought-tolerant. Fill the central cup.
care.fertilizer: Monthly during growing season.

name: Caladium
description: A stunning tropical plant with large, colorful heart-shaped leaves in reds, pinks, and whites.

care.water: Keep soil moist; water every 1–2 days.
care.sunlight: Bright indirect light; direct sun burns leaves.
care.fertilizer: Monthly during growing season.

name: Daisy
description: Cheerful white flowers with yellow centers. A symbol of innocence and purity.

care.water: Water regularly; keep soil moist but not waterlogged.
care.fertilizer: Monthly with balanced fertilizer.

name: Traveler's Palm
description: A dramatic architectural plant with paddle-shaped leaves arranged in a single plane like a fan.

care.water: Water regularly; moisture-loving.
care.fertilizer: Monthly during growing season.

name: Rosemary
description: An aromatic woody herb used in Mediterranean cooking and as a garden ornamental.

care.water: Drought-tolerant; water every 1–2 weeks; avoid overwatering.
care.fertilizer: Light feeding; half-strength balanced fertilizer in spring.

name: Taro
description: A tropical root vegetable with large, elephant-ear-like leaves. A staple crop in Asia.

care.water: Needs abundant water; grows in waterlogged or flooded conditions.
care.fertilizer: Monthly balanced fertilizer.

name: African Violet
description: A compact flowering houseplant with velvety leaves and purple, pink, or white blooms.

care.water: Water from below; keep leaves dry to prevent spotting.
care.sunlight: Bright indirect light; avoid direct sun.
care.fertilizer: Bi-weekly with African violet fertilizer.

name: Jackfruit Tree
description: The largest fruit-bearing tree, producing massive fruits. Popular in Southeast Asian cuisine.

care.water: Regular watering; drought-tolerant once mature.
care.fertilizer: Monthly balanced fertilizer when young.

name: Philodendron
description: A beloved houseplant with heart-shaped, glossy leaves. Easy-care and air-purifying.

care.water: Water when top inch of soil is dry.
care.fertilizer: Monthly in spring and summer.

name: Water Lily
description: Beautiful floating aquatic flowers that bloom on the water surface in ponds and lakes.

care.water: Fully aquatic; grown in still or slow-moving water.
care.soil: Heavy clay or loam submerged in water.
care.fertilizer: Aquatic plant tablets pushed into the soil.

name: Malunggay / Moringa
description: A miracle tree packed with nutrients. Leaves, pods, and seeds are all edible and medicinal.

care.water: Drought-tolerant; water during establishment and dry spells.
care.fertilizer: Minimal; grows in poor soil.

name: Crown of Thorns
description: A thorny succulent shrub with tiny, bright flowers. Drought-tolerant and long-blooming.

care.water: Allow soil to dry between waterings; every 2–3 weeks.
care.sunlight: Full sun to bright indirect light.
care.fertilizer: Monthly with balanced fertilizer during growing season.
